title: OSS dogfood found Kage edge cases on vercel ai
summary: Dogfooding Kage on vercel/ai found three product gaps: root init/recall can fail on broken symlinks because README.md is a symlink whose target includes a newline; package level propose/pr check can generate root relativ
body:
Dogfooding Kage on vercel/ai found three product gaps: root init/recall can fail on broken symlinks because README.md is a symlink whose target includes a newline; package-level propose/pr-check can generate root-relative changed paths then validate them against the subdirectory project and mark its own packet stale; package-level refresh indexed ignored build output after type-check produced packages/ai/dist, so generated/ignored artifacts need stronger exclusion in package projects.
Evidence: kage init/recall at repo root failed with ENOENT stat README.md; packages/ai kage pr check reported workflow-change-memory-main paths missing; after pnpm type-check, packages/ai/dist existed and Kage repo structure referenced dist/internal/index.test.js until dist was removed and refresh reran.
Verified by: Dogfood run for vercel/ai PR #15154 on 2026-05-11

risks: all referenced paths are missing: mcp/src, kage
